Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: They have two parts normative process behavior strict rules and desiderata recommended but not strict They provide an event log in tabular format for three cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We must identify normative violations The normative sequence Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Inform Applicant If Approved Finalize Documentation Close Case Also ordering constraints Credit Check before Risk Assessment Risk Assessment before Loan Decision Inform Applicant after Loan Decision If approved Finalize Documentation before Close If rejected do not finalize documentation before closing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In case 1 Sequence Application Received 0900 Credit Check 0930 Risk Assessment 1000 Loan Decision Approved 1100 Inform Applicant 1300 Finalize Documentation 1330 Close Case 1400 All constraints satisfied

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Check also that Credit Check before Risk Assessment yes Risk Assessment before Loan Decision yes Inform Applicant after Loan Decision yes For approved Finalize Documentation before Close yes So case 1 meets all normative rules

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Application Received 0800 Risk Assessment 0820 Credit Check 0900 Loan Decision Rejected 0930 Finalize Documentation 1000 Close Case 1030 There is no Inform Applicant at all normative says after loan decision you must inform applicant That is missing Also Credit Check must occur before Risk Assessment here it is reversed Also if loan is rejected you must not finalize documentation before closing but they do finalize documentation after a rejection

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus case 2 violates normative The ordering constraint Credit Check before Risk Assessment is violated after loan decision must inform applicant and finalization after rejection is disallowed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence Application Received 0900 Credit Check 14 Jan 1000 Risk Assessment 14 Jan 1100 Loan Decision Approved 14 Jan 1200 Inform Applicant 16 Jan 1500 Finalize Documentation 17 Jan 0900 Close Case 17 Jan 1000 Check ordering Credit Check before Risk Assessment yes Risk Assessment before Loan Decision yes Inform Applicant after Loan Decision yes Finalize Documentation before Close yes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So case 3 meets normative There is no violation of normative ordering They also did not do a Follow up Call but that is a desideratum not normative

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now second part desiderata They are recommended but not strict It is preferred that Credit Check is completed within 24 hours after application It is also recommended that the applicant is informed within 48 hours of loan decision It is encouraged but not required to perform a Follow up Call before closing the case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 credit check 30 minutes after application within 24 hours loan decision at 1100 inform at 1300 same day within 48 hours no follow up call recorded

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So case 1 has no timing desiderata violation but misses the optional follow up call which is a minor suboptimality

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 credit check at 0900 after application at 0800 within 24 hours but they never informed applicant so they do not meet the prompt informing recommendation They also did not do a follow up call

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For case 2 the lack of informing is more serious as a normative violation but it also means the desideratum of prompt informing is not met

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 credit check at 2024 01 14 1000 versus application at 2024 01 12 0900 which is about 49 hours later greater than 24 hours Loan decision at 2024 01 14 1200 inform at 2024 01 16 1500 which is about 51 hours later greater than 48 hours There is also no Follow up Call

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So case 3 fails the 24 hour credit check recommendation the 48 hour informing recommendation and the optional follow up call desideratum while still remaining normatively compliant

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Interpretation for case 2 could be operational oversight data entry error or a workflow mistake The finalization after rejection may reflect a mistaken use of a standard closure template

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Interpretation for case 3 could be resource constraints backlog or external delays in obtaining information These would explain late credit check and late applicant notification without implying a hard rule breach

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Interpretation for missing follow up calls across cases may be that staff treated the call as optional and deprioritized it because it is only a recommended best practice
